steps:
Total sweets initially = 12 red + 9 blue + 6 green + 3 yellow = 30 sweets.
Priya picks first sweet and eats it. It is red, so one red sweet is removed.
After first pick: red sweets = 11, blue sweets = 9, green sweets = 6, yellow sweets = 3. Total sweets left = 11+9+6+3 = 29.
We want probability second sweet is NOT blue.
Number of non-blue sweets left = red (11) + green (6) + yellow (3) = 20.
Probability = 20/29.
Check simplification: 20/29 is already in simplest form.
Now compare with options:
A) 7/10 = 0.7, 20/29 ≈ 0.6897 — not equal.
B) 2/3 ≈ 0.6667 — not equal.
C) 7/29 ≈ 0.2414 — not equal.
D) 21/29 ≈ 0.7241 — not equal to 20/29.
Wait — I made a mistake: 20/29 is not among options. Re-check calculation: non-blue sweets = total sweets left (29) minus blue sweets left (9) = 20. So probability = 20/29.
Option D is 21/29, which is different. Did I miscount total after first pick? After removing one red: red = 11, blue = 9, green = 6, yellow = 3. Total = 11+9+6+3 = 29. Correct.
